ddls:
CREATE TABLE "experiments" (
  "experiment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each experiment */,
  "experiment_name" TEXT /* Name of the experiment */,
  "experiment_type" TEXT /* Type of experiment (e.g., microbial growth, enzyme kinetics) */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the experiment */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the experiment */,
  "protocol_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the protocol document */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* User who created the experiment */,
  "last_modified_by" INTEGER /* User who last modified the experiment */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the experiment */,
  "version" INTEGER /* Version number of the experiment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("experiment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "samples" (
  "sample_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sample */,
  "experiment_id" INTEGER /* ID of the experiment the sample belongs to */,
  "sample_name" TEXT /* Name of the sample */,
  "sample_description" TEXT /* Description of the sample */,
  "sample_type" TEXT /* Type of the sample */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the sample */,
  "storage_location" TEXT /* Location where the sample is stored */,
  "collection_date" TEXT /* Date when the sample was collected */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sample_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_samples_experiment_id FOREIGN KEY ("experiment_id") REFERENCES experiments ("experiment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "replicates" (
  "replicate_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each replicate */,
  "sample_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sample the replicate belongs to */,
  "replicate_number" INTEGER /* Number of the replicate */,
  "condition" TEXT /* Specific conditions under which the replicate was tested */,
  "outcome" TEXT /* Result or outcome of the replicate */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("replicate_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_replicates_sample_id FOREIGN KEY ("sample_id") REFERENCES samples ("sample_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "measurements" (
  "measurement_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each measurement */,
  "replicate_id" INTEGER /* ID of the replicate the measurement belongs to */,
  "temperature" REAL /* Temperature of the measurement */,
  "time" INTEGER /* Time of the measurement */,
  "od" REAL /* Optical density of the measurement */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement */,
  "measurement_type" TEXT /* Type of measurement */,
  "instrument_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the instrument used for the measurement */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("measurement_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_measurements_replicate_id FOREIGN KEY ("replicate_id") REFERENCES replicates ("replicate_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, data analyst, admin) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for user authentication */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department to which the user belongs */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date and time of the last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "experiment_id" INTEGER /* ID of the experiment being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the experiment */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the experiment was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent of the browser or application used for access */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_experiment_id FOREIGN KEY ("experiment_id") REFERENCES experiments ("experiment_id")
)
column_names: Type of experiment (e.g., microbial growth, enzyme kinetics)
User who last modified the experiment
ID of the experiment the sample belongs to
Location where the sample is stored
Date when the sample was collected
ID of the sample the replicate belongs to
Specific conditions under which the replicate was tested

ID of the replicate the measurement belongs to

Department to which the user belongs
